explicit_text: I was born in San Pedro, California, on June 12, 1958, and grew up in a nice community near the beach. My family moved there in the early 1950s because my father loved surfing and the ocean. San Pedro was where I started my early jobs, including working on the docks and eventually getting involved with the maritime industry.

explicit_text: My father was half Argentinian and half Armenian, which gave me a mixed cultural heritage. Although he was born in downtown Los Angeles, his Argentinian roots were part of our family background. This blend of cultures was part of my upbringing, even though we lived in California.

explicit_text: My father was half Armenian, and our family was part of a big Armenian enclave of tire dealers in Los Angeles. I learned the ropes through this union, doing tasks like driving UTRs and shoveling. The ILWU became a major part of my working life as I transitioned from seasonal boat work to longshoreman duties.

explicit_text: I worked for Catalina Cruises as a boat operator starting when I was 21, after getting my license. I was the youngest first operator they ever had and worked there for about fifteen years, running ferries to Catalina Island and doing harbor charters and whale watches. It was a job I loved because it combined my passion for the ocean with my work.

The island was a key part of my maritime career and love of the ocean.
